summary: This brief post announces the publication of an Alt Banking group essay in the Huffington Post about hopes for the next Attorney General. The piece introduces the concept of 'marble columns' as the inverse of 'broken windows' policing—describing how corporate employees enjoy immunity from prosecution. O'Neil also announces a guest blogger will cover while she travels to Haiti.
key_points: The Alt Banking group published 'With Economic Justice For All' in Huffington Post
The essay coins 'marble columns' as the opposite of 'broken windows' policing
'Marble columns' refers to corporate immunity—never getting arrested if you work at prestigious firms
The piece advocates for economic justice from the incoming Attorney General
money_quotes: Instead of getting arrested for nothing, you never get arrested, as long as you work at a company with marble columns.
key_insight: The 'marble columns' concept captures the two-tiered justice system where corporate employees enjoy immunity while others face aggressive policing for minor infractions.
